idea.topic: Interstellar Travel to Proxima Centauri b
idea.hook: Dream of visiting an alien world like Proxima Centauri b?
facts: Proxima Centauri b is our closest known exoplanet, just 4.24 light-years away.
If you could travel at light speed (299,792 km/s), the trip would take ~4.24 years.
Our fastest spacecraft, Voyager 1, travels at ~17 km/s. At this speed, the journey would take a staggering ~74,400 years!
idea.payoff: Interstellar travel means waiting for generations... or revolutionary propulsion!
idea.title: Proxima b: How Long To Get There? ⏳👽

scenes.text: Dream of visiting Proxima Centauri b?
scenes.visual: A stunning, artistic rendering of an alien exoplanet (Proxima Centauri b) slowly rotating, with a reddish star in the background. Stars twinkle around it.

scenes.text: Our closest exoplanet: just 4.24 light-years away.
scenes.visual: An animated infographic showing two star systems (Sun and Proxima Centauri) with a glowing line connecting them, labeled '4.24 Light-Years'.

scenes.text: At Light Speed (299,792 km/s), the trip would take ~4.24 years.
scenes.visual: A visual representation of light streaking across space from Earth to Proxima Centauri b, with a digital timer rapidly counting up to '4.24 years'.
A stylized 3D model of the Voyager 1 spacecraft slowly drifting through a vast star field. The '74,400 years!' text appears large and impactful.

scenes.text: Interstellar travel: Generations... or revolutionary propulsion!
scenes.visual: A dynamic transition from a classic sailing ship (representing generational travel) fading into a futuristic, conceptual spacecraft (e.g., a warp drive effect or laser sail) heading towards distant stars.
